quickStart.title: ⚡ Quick Start
quickStart.subtitle: From zero to your first subtitle
quickStart.description: WhisperFlow Studio turns the speech in your videos into subtitle files, saved right next to the originals. The core flow is just three steps: Import → Scan → Transcribe.

dragDrop.label: Two ways to import
dragDrop.body: Click the Browse… button on the Main tab to pick a folder, or drag a folder directly onto the Media Directory card in the left panel.

fileTypes.label: Supported formats
fileTypes.body: Video: mp4 / mkv / mov / avi / m4v / ts; audio: mp3 / wav / m4a / flac / ogg / aiff.

recursive.label: Recursive scan
recursive.body: Files in subfolders are included. Videos that already have a matching .srt or .vtt sidecar are automatically skipped.

prepare.title: 2. Prepare your environment (first-time setup)
prepare.intro: There's a small one-time setup before the first run. Subsequent launches are ready to go.

model.label: Download a Whisper model
model.body: Open the Models tab and pick one to download. For newcomers, small (fast, ~465 MB) or medium (better quality, ~1.5 GB) are good starting points. For Chinese / Japanese audio, go straight to large-v2 (~2.9 GB).

ffmpeg.label: Verify ffmpeg is installed
ffmpeg.body: If the System Check panel says ffmpeg not found, click the Install ffmpeg button next to it — a themed dialog will detect Homebrew / Scoop / winget and install in one click.

python.label: Python virtualenv
python.body: Created automatically the first time you click Run. You can also hit Detect now next to pythonPath in Settings to verify a compatible Python 3.10+ is reachable.

settings.title: 3. Tune transcription parameters
settings.intro: Open the Settings tab — every field has an inline description. The important ones:

model.label: model
model.body: Pick one you've already downloaded from the Models tab. Bigger models are more accurate but slower.

language.label: language
language.body: Leave blank to auto-detect. Specifying a language usually gives more stable results.

vad.label: vad
vad.body: Recommended: silero-vad-skip-gaps. It filters out silent regions and dramatically speeds up transcription. Setting this to none sends the entire file to Whisper.

initialPrompt.label: initial_prompt
initialPrompt.body: A priming string fed to Whisper. Picking a language auto-fills a sensible default; you can also write your own (e.g. domain-specific terminology).

scan.label: Scan first
scan.body: Click Scan for Missing Subtitles. The app lists every file in the folder that still needs subtitles.

runBtn.label: Start transcription
runBtn.body: Click Run Transcription. The first queued file starts processing. Live progress shows up in the Batch Progress card below.

controls.label: In-flight controls
controls.body: Hit Pause to pause, Skip Current to move past the current file, or Stop Batch to abort everything.

autoLoop.label: Auto-loop mode
autoLoop.body: Enable to automatically move on to the next file when one finishes — perfect for unattended batch processing of a whole folder.

location.label: Where do subtitle files go?
location.body: Right next to the original video, in the same folder. Same filename, new extensions: .srt / .vtt / .txt / .json.

history.label: Recent transcripts
history.body: The Recent Transcripts list at the bottom of the Main tab tracks the last 10. Click Show in Finder to open the folder directly.

reveal.label: Open folder directly
reveal.body: When the Next to Transcribe card is visible, click Show in Finder to jump straight to the file's folder.

multilingual.a: Yes. Whisper natively supports 99 languages; just set the language field in Settings.

offline.a: Only the first time, to download the Whisper model. After that, transcription is fully offline.

concurrent.q: Can it process multiple files at once?
concurrent.a: One at a time — but enabling Auto-loop mode makes it automatically continue to the next file.

gpu.a: Linux / Windows will automatically use CUDA if available. macOS currently runs on CPU.

privacy.a: No — everything is processed locally. Apart from the one-time model download, there's no outbound network activity.

runGrey.q: Scan worked, but Run is still greyed out — why?
runGrey.a: Check the System Check panel on the left — there's usually a blocking issue like venv not initialised or ffmpeg not found that needs fixing first.

python.label: Python 3 not found
python.body: Go to Settings and click Detect now next to pythonPath. If it still can't find one, specify a path to your Python 3.10+ executable manually.

ffmpeg.label: ffmpeg / ffprobe not found
ffmpeg.body: Click the Install ffmpeg button in System Check. Or install manually: brew install ffmpeg (macOS), scoop install ffmpeg (Windows), sudo apt install ffmpeg (Linux).

modelSlow.label: Model download is slow
modelSlow.body: The first large-v2 download is about 2.9 GB — expect a few to fifteen minutes depending on your connection. Subsequent runs are fully offline.

timingOff.label: Subtitle timing is off
timingOff.body: Check the vad_padding setting (default: 1s). Increase it to avoid clipping the start of utterances; decrease it to hug the raw speech more tightly.

errorBanner.label: Red error banner appeared
errorBanner.body: Click Show details on the banner to open the full error message with an error code — useful for searching or reporting the issue.

welcome.title: Welcome to WhisperFlow Studio
welcome.body: We'll walk you through your first transcription in 5 quick steps. You can skip the tour at any time.

step1.title: Step 1: Choose a media folder
step1.body: Click the Browse… button to pick a folder of videos or audio files. WhisperFlow recursively finds every file that still needs subtitles.

step2.title: Step 2: Download a Whisper model
step2.body: On your first run, open the Models tab and download a model. small or medium are good starting points.

step3.title: Step 3: Scan for missing subtitles
step3.body: Click Scan to build a queue of files that still need transcription. The step guide on the right summarises the flow too.

step4.title: Step 4: Start transcription
step4.body: Click Run to process the first queued file. You can pause, skip, or stop at any time from the progress card below.

step5.title: Step 5: Monitor progress and find results
step5.body: Live progress appears here. Finished subtitles are saved right next to each video (as .srt / .vtt). You can replay this tour anytime via the ? button in the titlebar.
